acceptance_targets: [ch12-booker-august-leverage-underused] PASS if, after the August scene, at least one later action or line in the Hester/Thea/Cora sequence explicitly turns on his posted notice or his new social leverage; FAIL if the visit can still be removed without materially changing the household argument beyond the fact that a sign was hung.

[ch12-craft-scrubbed-pain-voice] PASS if each cited beat gives the reader either Dell's actual words or a concrete rendered sound/body action tied to the moment; FAIL if any of those beats still relies on summary tags such as 'he cursed,' 'he swore,' or 'half groan, half curse' to carry the pressure.

[ch12-dialogue-aphorism-drift] PASS if the revised exchange grounds at least three major turns in immediate scene facts such as Dell's fever, planks, wages, train travel, cough, or the basin lip, and at least one speaker interrupts or revises herself mid-thought; FAIL if the scene still runs on consecutive abstract declarations about truth, leaving, loyalty, or burial.

[ch12-prose-negation-tic] PASS if no more than one of the cited passages uses a serial corrective-negation stack; FAIL if two or more of those moments still rely on the same 'Not X. Not Y. Only Z.' rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after the August scene, at least one later action or line in the Hester/Thea/Cora sequence explicitly turns on his posted notice or his new social leverage; FAIL if the visit can still be removed without materially changing the household argument beyond the fact that a sign was hung.
findings.evidence: August clocks the household's exposed geometry and arrives with formal closure language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:215, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259, but once he leaves at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:271 the chapter spends little of that new leverage before moving to Cora's turn at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:475 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:489.
findings.problem: The August visit introduces two potent pressures, public closure of the basin and his sharpened suspicion about Cora and Thea, but the chapter barely cashes either in. That makes the mid-chapter escalation feel more like reminder than consequence, and the Hester/Thea scene has to regenerate urgency the August beat should have already intensified.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either compress the August scene beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:205 if it is only meant to restate closure, or rework the aftermath anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:423, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:475 so his visit materially changes later behavior, timing, or risk inside the house.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each cited beat gives the reader either Dell's actual words or a concrete rendered sound/body action tied to the moment; FAIL if any of those beats still relies on summary tags such as 'he cursed,' 'he swore,' or 'half groan, half curse' to carry the pressure.
findings.evidence: At several pain peaks the prose narrates around Dell's voice instead of rendering it: the lifted sound at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:55, the summary curse at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:63, the generalized swearing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:141, and the 'half groan, half curse' placeholder at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:377.
findings.problem: This is sanitization drift. The chapter is admirably specific about bile, fever, urination, and humiliation, then repeatedly blinks at the exact sounds and words Dell would make under that pressure. Summarizing the cry or curse instead of letting us hear it softens the scene right where the constitution asks for raw specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the stress beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:377 so Dell's pain and shame arrive as exact utterance, broken phrase, or concrete physical sound/action, not as narrated placeholders like 'cursed' or 'half groan, half curse.'

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange grounds at least three major turns in immediate scene facts such as Dell's fever, planks, wages, train travel, cough, or the basin lip, and at least one speaker interrupts or revises herself mid-thought; FAIL if the scene still runs on consecutive abstract declarations about truth, leaving, loyalty, or burial.
findings.evidence: The Hester/Thea kitchen argument stacks polished thematic declarations at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:345, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:351, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:361, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:365, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:367.
findings.problem: The exchange starts from strong character pressure, then drifts into maxim-speech. Hester and Thea remain distinguishable in broad outline, but too many of their highest-stakes lines arrive as finished thematic pronouncements rather than interrupted, situational speech, which blunts the scene's realism and makes the argument sound more authored than lived.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retune the confrontation around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:341, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:345, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:351, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:361, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:367 so Hester argues from spent breath, field memory, and practical basin talk, while Thea argues from immediate transport, money, school, and Dell's condition; break the polish with interruptions, self-revisions, or local detail.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the cited passages uses a serial corrective-negation stack; FAIL if two or more of those moments still rely on the same 'Not X. Not Y. Only Z.' rhythm.
findings.evidence: A corrective negation formula recurs enough to become visible: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:95 ('Not alarm. Not comfort either. Only accounting.'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:261 ('Not mercy. Not even decency.'), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:493 ('Not a promise. Not even agreement. Only measurement.').
findings.problem: The repeated 'Not X. Not Y. Only Z.' construction reads as a chapter-level tic rather than a fresh rhythmic choice. Because each instance lands at a moment of judgment, the repetition starts to sound like authorial machinery instead of Cora's live perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep at most one instance of this negation stack and recast the others at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:95, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:493 into different syntax that is specific to the local perception rather than the same corrective template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into queer secrecy, erotic specificity, dust-sick bodies, economic shame, bad bargains, and the humiliations of dependence. When deciding between a tidier scene and a truer one, choose the version that preserves historical pressure and emotional contradiction even if it leaves characters compromised, unsympathetic, or exposed.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If grief, desire, debt, illness, bodily damage, or shame are abstracted at the moment they should become sensory and social, rewrite toward the concrete consequence until the scene stops hiding from itself.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: moderate-high - contractions are the baseline for speech; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, social performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural rhythms across the book: survival set-piece, aftermath inventory, intimate chamber scene, bureaucratic squeeze, outdoor search, quiet communal ritual, preparation without payoff, public reckoning, and departure elegy. Vary scene counts, dialogue density, and where the turn comes from so consecutive chapters never feel built from the same dramatic machine.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken and period-credible, with agricultural, domestic, and biblical undertones used sparingly; favor exact nouns and verbs over decorative frontier lyricism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person rotating among Cora, Thea, Dell, August, and Hester, with only brief collective widening when weather or communal memory demands it
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive prose: longer weather and labor sentences that can suddenly snap short at revelation, pain, or shame.
